channel.title: Comments for page "The Shape of a Gun"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "The Shape of a Gun"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just popping in to shout my appreciation! After the first couple Series of SCPs I recognized the subtext of the Foundation as slavers, racists (pure human superiority) and tyrants (unelected O5s control the world) - Nazis, in a word. So happy to see them fought against! This tale just lights me up with excitement! I figure it should have at least one 2020 comment. Thank you for this!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: it makes my fucking day that people are talking about worm

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The story and characters wear their anime influences a little too obviously for my taste, but the writing is very good indeed. In the opening section in particular it is excellent.

I would love a sequel that explored what the nature of Monica’s powers signifies for the symbiote, and for her approach to problem-solving generally.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Beautiful. This world feels alive. There’s pain and regret, and necessary cruelty, but every character feels like a real person.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shit I loved that series!!! The tinkerers were my favorites. Almost no one references that series anymore.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: fantastic stuff! i really like norman

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow. It could be horrible tale, a shitty X-man fanfic. You, good sir/madame, made it excellent. Monica is my new favourite D-class,

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's funny, because to my recollection, Steelheart was a pretty blatant rip off of Darker than Black.

Oh, and kudos Cadaver for making a kickass story. I do feel like it strays quite a bit from my personal conception of the Foundation— it's cool but lacks that grim clinical feel I've grown to expect. Nonetheless, +1 for a great tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Apparently Monica has a page on VS Battles wiki! Armando as well, lol.

https://vsbattles.wikia.com/wiki/Monica_Pinkston_(SCP_Foundation)

https://vsbattles.wikia.com/wiki/SCP-3589

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now that the doomsday contest has ended, i've started reading through some more of the entries and this really is amazing. It's also really unique, It doesn't read like anything else i've read on the site. It sounds like an excerpt from the middle of a novel, almost.

I really do think this has huge potential - time to binge read all the other articles from apotheosis!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, but the sex got pretty egregious at the end, especially considering the age of those… involved

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really excellently written. Very engaging.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So we've got a buffed Miss Militia, a Gaster-type guy… Eh. The prose also felt a bit flat for some reason I can't articulate. My sleep deprived brain might just be doing something weird, but I'm not sure I like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Right on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A crater left over from some teenage catastrophe.

This has incredible characterization all the way through. There's substance here to back up all the style.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted when introduced to Norman.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gotcha, thanks for clearing that up. And yes, "boxing" is a great term we should see more of!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
how do mutants and non-mutants share a community when 3396 is contagious?

The symbiote spreads like wildfire, but the actual effects can take months or maybe even years to show up. So they're living as normals and mutants side by side, but they're all compromised by the anomaly, so the Foundation will box them all if given the chance.

Boxing someone is the super cool term I just made up for throwing people in containment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"foundation is stupid" tropes aside. This is an amazing piece

Also, how do mutants and non-mutants share a community when 3396 is contagious? (or did I misunderstand and old vegas is just mutants?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why are the Foundation so idiotic handling this? yeah its the end of the world, but with this kind of firepower, you could always ally with them, have them be a sovereign city-state in the Orange Zone. It would be more effecient and save more lives to ally with sympathetic and/or neutral towns to retake earth.
+1 for eldritch Norman

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
YES THANK YOU, CreativeUsername. That is EXACTLY how I fell about this.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It’s the only YA series that ever held my attention until the end, so clearly it got something right.

Also as far as I remember there was very little sex.
Monica's got the celebratory fireworks already set up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added navigation links to the rest of our team's series and set 3396 as a parent page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How to tell if you're reading a CadaverCommander tale - A How-To Guide

  • A mayor who doesn't want to be mayor but still tries their best anyways: Check
  • A very large and scary character who's really a sweet heart: Check
  • A township that's falling apart at the seams: Check
  • A strong theme of freedom, generally from the confines of the Foundation: Check
  • An incredibly metal story: Check

My main thought when reading this was "This is fucknuts but with mutants, and less childishness", but I'd be lying if I said that was a bad thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Omg I wish I was even half as good at writing as you are just so I could explain how happy this tale made me.
This blew my mind, please write more ^_^

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it's implied that shits about to hit the fan for the mutants, but until then, your points stand. It did get a bit too xman-y for my taste, but not enough to merit a downvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know, minus a stupid and unnecessary amount of sex, pointless red herrings that never get cleared up, a plot that breaks its own rules, and shitty, 8th grade writing. Other than that, yeah, exactly like Gone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

The more of your works I read the more I'm impressed with how well you can write likeable and sympathetic characters. Whether it's a heavy metal zombie or a disembodied mass of mind reading flesh you're simply fantastic at writing characters I enjoy. Keep doing what you do cadaver.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shame

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But Seras is a vampire and can't make guns out of her body…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy crap, I see what all the hype was about. This is simply stupendous. I had been hoping SCP-3396 would lead to X-men shenanigans, and boy oh boy did you deliver.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, Cadaver I cannot physically describe the ecstasy I have after reading that. So if you haven't read them there is a book series called Steelheart by Brandon Sanderson. It is quite frankly this exact thing. However when I first went into this I was like "Oh, it's just ripping of Steelheart." How fucking wrong I was. The execution on this was phenomenal, neigh, perfect. You went the extra mile with the formatting. Dialogue had few flaws. Honestly this is perhaps the best tale I have ever read on this site. Good fucking job.

If I could +2 this I would.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvoted.

I didn’t really care for this, I mean, the writing was of course fantastic, the visuals and flow alone would warrant an upvote, but it’s the premise that really kills it for me.

My problem with it is that it’s like the X-Men, as Dead Moth pointed out, but for me it’s a negative. The X-Men are superheroes, ones I don’t care for at that, and I don’t come to the SCP foundation to read about superheroes. I come to read about strange, sometimes horrific, phenomena and objects, and this is neither, besides maybe Norman for strangeness. What I mean is that the characters are nothing but humans with superpowers fighting an evil shadow organization which just happens to be the Foundation in this case.

Perhaps if humans affected by the virus did suffer some shift in mental state or a loss in humanity I’d like it better, as it’d be something to change this from a regular superhero story, but as it is it’s just that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We all know this is really just a Hellsing fanfic in disguise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Instant classic, seriously. I really hope this gets expanded after the contest, because you’ve created a rich and compelling group in this tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've been trying not to vote on contest articles yet, or really even read them, given how many there are and how I'm still working on my own. But I ended up reading this due to the title (I've not read anything else from your team at all), and well, you've written one of the best tales of 2018 so I don't quite know how many others will stack up. Also, you literally wouldn't even need to write a sequel to this for me to love it. This could be a standalone and you could write some totally other different story and I'd still dig into it as hard as I do with this.

Have I inflated your ego enough yet? Because I'm holding back the temptation to do more of that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fan-fucking-tastic.

In 2 articles, you managed to grow D-77777 from a nameless anomaly to Boomgirl and now, Monica The Boom Queen. You have a true talent for creating lovable characters and entertaining human interactions with very un-human subjects.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Brb, designing hundreds of my own OC mutants because this is a fucking brilliant premise with wonderful characters and it should be illegal for you to write something this good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconded. In fact, once I'd gotten over the sheer brilliance of it, this is the worst bit of the whole thing — the possibility that we might not get more of these characters. This needs to be a novel, or a series of novels, or a summer blockbuster film. It's too fantastic to leave alone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Norman is perhaps my favorite character on this site now. Kudos. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reminds me of the Gone series with people randomly developing superpowers and struggling to keep a society of mutants and non-mutants going.

Great stuff +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is like the X-men of the SCP-Foundation, finally done right. If this is expanded upon properly, it could be the next big GoI.

Take your +1.

On another note, (possibly on the expansion of this group), If the SCP doesn't typically alter mental states, there is no reason to believe the foundation wouldn't use it on, say, some of their most loyal agents or strike-team members.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Norman hovered eldritch and horrible above them all, gazing downward with his hundreds of eyes, feeding the Queen the commander's innermost plans as he thought them and providing the defenders the profound unity of being able to think with one mind.

I had to make a second post about this quote. The imagery that this puts in my mind is absolutely orgasmic and incredibly detailed. I actually hope this turns into something more than just one tale. It's too phenomenal to remain that way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Probably one of the best tales on this site. You're a mastermind, Cadaver.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I understand firepower.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yep this is great. So much to like here I don't know where to start.
I'm a little jealous actually because I had gotten it into my head to write a very similar concept to this, with some event or another giving a bunch of people weird powers and very publicly breaking the masquerade, and it looks like you've beaten me to it.
I can see this setting lasting long past the doomsday contest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I desperately wanted to come in and quote a single line of this that I adore for how well written it is, but there are just too damn many to choose from!

And I'm not even a third of the way through this…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is some Worm-tier shit. Holy fucking goddamn.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Monica may very well be my new favorite character on the site.

item.title: click click, boom
item.content:encoded:
a glimpse into the world after SCP-3396.

thank you for reading. ♥
